question_title: Will the interest in “tsa funding” change between 2026-04-01 and 2026-04-09 according to Google Trends?
summary: The Google Trends value for "tsa funding" on April 1 is 6, having returned to its pre-spike baseline (6-9) after peaking at 100 on March 27 during intense legislative drama. The forecast window (Apr 1-9) coincides with Congressional recess (until Apr 13), removing the primary catalyst for this legislative search term. TSA agents are being paid via Trump's executive order, easing the airport crisis that drove the spike. The "Doesn't change" band [3,9] covers the entire normal baseline range. "Decreases" requires ≤2, nearly impossible at this baseline. The main upside risk is Easter travel (Apr 5) during the ongoing DHS shutdown, but "tsa funding" is a political/legislative term unlikely to spike from travel-related coverage. With no congressional action possible during recess and the immediate crisis easing, baseline stability is the dominant outcome.

factors.description: Value at baseline floor (6): Pre-spike baseline was 6-9, and 'Doesn't change' covers [3,9] which encompasses all normal baseline values. 'Decreases' requires <=2 which is near-impossible at this baseline.

Congress in recess until April 13: No legislative votes, no floor drama, no new funding bills during the entire Apr 1-9 window. The primary driver of search spikes (legislative action) is completely absent.

TSA agents getting paid via Trump executive order: The immediate crisis (unpaid workers, massive airport delays) is easing. PBS reported Mar 30 that airport bottlenecks are easing. Reduced urgency = less search interest.
Post-spike decay complete: Value dropped from 100 (Mar 27) to 6 (Apr 1) in 5 days, fully returning to pre-spike baseline. The story's peak intensity has passed.
Easter travel (April 5) during ongoing DHS shutdown: Could generate media coverage, but 'tsa funding' is a political/legislative search term - travelers search 'TSA wait times' not 'tsa funding'.
Potential legal/political developments around Trump's executive spending order: Constitutionality of bypassing Congress could generate news. But speculative - no specific challenge filed.
Decreases requires value <= 2: Near-complete disappearance of searches needed. Even the lowest value in the date range was 6. Essentially impossible without topic ceasing to exist.
SerpAPI measurement uncertainty: Resolution API may differ from pytrends by 1-3 points. At value 6, this could shift the outcome but more likely keeps it within the ±3 band.

reasoning: The reflection passed. Let me finalize.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the interest in “tsa funding” change between 2026-04-01 and 2026-04-09 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43016)
[TSA shutdown: GOP leaders Thune and Johnson boost two-track approach to funding DHS](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/01/tsa-shutdown-dhs-homeland.html)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=tsa%20funding&date=2026-03-10%202026-04-09](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=tsa%20funding&date=2026-03-10%202026-04-09) for 2026-04-09 is within 3 of the value at 2026-04-01. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "tsa funding",
  "date": "2026-03-10 2026-04-09",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
